title: Teeth Polishing -- Technique or Treatment -- Engine-Driven Devices
content: These are widely used and need either a straight or a contra-angled handpiece. A polishing brush or a rubber cup is attached to the handpiece used at a 2500 to 3000 rpm velocity. It is estimated that the average speed dental hygienists use is 2500 rpm. [15] The motion used for polishing in clinical practice is patting motion, and the device should be a slow-speed handpiece always rotated at the lowest rpm. The rubber cups or the polishing brushes are either autoclavable or disposable. Most tooth surfaces require 2 to 5 seconds to get polished, with the rubber cup contacting the tooth for 4.5 seconds. title: Hand Tendon Transfers -- Indications
content: Ulnar claw hand occurs because of unopposed extension forces by the EDC and EDM on the small and ring finger MP joints with no counter flexion force by the interossei and ulnar two lumbricals innervated by the ulnar nerve. The extended posture of the MP joint prevents extension forces from the EDC from being transmitted to the PIP and DIP joints. Also, the normal extensor force to the PIP and DIP joints through the extensor hood of the ring and small fingers is deficient secondary to weakness of the interossei and ulnar two lumbricals. A low ulnar nerve palsy results in a more severe claw hand because the FDP to the ring and small fingers are intact, worsening the imbalance of flexion forces across the PIP and DIP joints.

title: Volar Splinting -- Technique or Treatment -- Splinting Technique
content: The side of the extremity contralateral to the fracture should be measured to calculate the necessary length of the casting material. When creating a plaster splint, fold the plaster into layers and dip it into the container of water at an angle of 45 degrees. Completely immerse the plaster. Once the bubbling ceases, the plaster may be removed from the water. Squeeze out the excess water using longitudinal compression massage. If the splinting material is too long, the edges may be folded over to achieve the desired length. content: Delivery by double application of forceps and manual rotation of fetal head are obstetric interventions that are performed directly on the fetal head. These interconnected procedures are important tools for managing difficult deliveries and optimizing outcomes for both mother and infant.

title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2166
content: Intrauterine pregnancy is clinically linked to several obstetric interventions and outcomes. It often leads to the use of labor induction (تحفيز المخاض) to initiate delivery when medically indicated, and in some cases delivery may be performed with forceps (الولادة بالملقط) to assist the birth process. Although intrauterine pregnancy itself signifies successful conception, certain underlying conditions or complications associated with the pregnancy can also be related to subsequent infertility (العقم), highlighting the need for careful evaluation of reproductive health before and after pregnancy. Understanding these interconnections helps clinicians anticipate appropriate labor management strategies and address potential fertility concerns in patient care.

title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2912
content: Pregnancy-Related Procedures is a broad category of obstetric interventions that encompasses specific techniques such as بضع الفرج and Vaginal Assisted Delivery, each classified as a child procedure within this group. Both بضع الفرج and Vaginal Assisted Delivery represent assisted vaginal delivery methods used to facilitate safe childbirth when spontaneous vaginal delivery is not optimal, highlighting the importance of tailored procedural options in maternal care.

title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2028
content: Malposition, disproportion or other labor complications are recognized as a form of failure to induce labor (فشل تحريض المخاض) and are also classified as causes of a prolonged first stage of labor (مرحلة أولى مطولة من المخاض) as well as a prolonged second stage of labor (مرحلة ثانية مطولة من المخاض). These classifications reflect that abnormal fetal positioning, size mismatch, or other intrapartum problems can impede the initiation of effective uterine contractions and can extend both early and later phases of the birthing process, highlighting the need for careful monitoring and appropriate obstetric interventions to prevent labor dystocia and improve maternal‑fetal outcomes.

title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 1 -- Community 185
content: Obstetric Procedure is defined as a hierarchical class that includes ante‑partum, intrapartum, and post‑partum interventions. Child categories comprise Antepartum Procedures (application, insertion, removal) such as fetal reduction, amniocentesis, placental biopsy, amnioinfusion, uterine curettage, and other placental surgeries; Intrapartum procedures such as vaginal delivery (with or without episiotomy or forceps), perineal incision, manual fetal rotation, intrapartum fetal stimulation, sweeping of membranes, artificial rupture of membranes (ARM) and its surgical subtype, cervical dilation/incision, obstetrical trachelotomy, force‑force delivery, removal of retained pregnancy products, and cardiac puncture; and Post‑partum obstetric operations including repair of obstetric laceration, severe delivery procedures, and immediate post‑delivery care.
